summary: Comprehensive testing of native WebRTC telehealth module. All 18 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend lobby loads correctly for both host and patient roles. In-meeting view with all 5 control buttons verified. AI Clinical Assistant panel toggles, symptoms input, and Gemini diagnose API all working correctly. getUserMedia fails in headless browser as expected — WebSocket mock + canvas stream used to test in-meeting view.

ui_bugs.issue: Gemini diagnosis response renders markdown `**bold**` syntax as raw text (e.g. '**Assessment Summary**' shows as literal asterisks). The result div uses `whitespace-pre-wrap` CSS but does NOT parse markdown. Consider using a markdown renderer (react-markdown) or pre-processing the response to strip/convert markdown markers.

issues: Camera preview area appears as a dark box (no camera preview in automated test env) — this is expected behavior when camera not available. No fallback UI (avatar/placeholder icon) shown when camera is unavailable.

action_items: MINOR: Render Gemini markdown in diagnosis-result div — use react-markdown or strip ** markers for cleaner output
OPTIONAL: Add camera-unavailable fallback in lobby preview (show avatar icon when getUserMedia fails)
critical_code_review_comments: video_rooms_routes.py uses in-memory `_signal_rooms` dict for WebSocket signaling. This will not scale across multiple server instances (stateless deployment) — for production, a shared signaling store (Redis pub/sub) would be needed. Acceptable for MVP.
WebSocket signaling has 120s timeout with ping keepalive — good for connection management.
gemini_routes.py: No auth protection on POST /api/gemini/diagnose endpoint — any unauthenticated user can call it. Should add bearer token requirement for production.
POST /api/video-rooms/meetings has no authentication requirement — publicly accessible meeting creation. Consider adding auth dependency.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all critical flows verified — minor markdown rendering issue in AI panel)

seed_data_creation: 3 TEST_ prefixed meetings created during backend testing (TEST_WebRTC Telehealth Consultation, TEST_Scheduled Meeting, TEST_Active Meeting Now)
context_for_next_testing_agent: WebRTC module fully tested. Test meeting ID: 72a2a36a-6a23-405a-81c5-b0506c185270. In-meeting view requires getUserMedia mock or real browser — use add_init_script to mock navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia + WebSocket before page load. All 5 control buttons (toggle-audio, toggle-video, toggle-screen, toggle-diagnose, leave-meeting) have correct data-testid. AI diagnose panel: data-testid=diagnose-panel, symptoms-input, diagnose-submit-btn, diagnosis-result. Backend: all CRUD + Gemini diagnose endpoints healthy.
